title.title: Test Result Trends: How to Analyze Regression Testing Results Over Time in ReportPortal

contentfulBlogPost.seoDescription: Use ReportPortal test reporting dashboards to monitor regression trends and detect quality degradation early.
contentfulBlogPost.author: ReportPortal Team

articleBody.raw: {"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Single test runs rarely tell the full story about product quality. A build can fail today and pass tomorrow – but what really matters is what happens over time. Are failures becoming more frequent? Are the same areas breaking again and again? Is product stability improving or slowly degrading?","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"This is where test result trends become far more valuable than isolated results.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"ReportPortal is designed not just to collect test outcomes, but also to support test automation analytics, helping teams analyze regression testing results over time, identify patterns, and make better quality decisions based on real data.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Beyond a single test run","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"A failed test run answers only one question:","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"\"Is something broken right now?\" It does not answer: ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Is this a new problem or an old one? ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Is the product improving or getting worse? ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Are failures random, or do they follow a pattern? ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"}],"nodeType":"unordered-list"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"When teams focus only on today’s results, they often: ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Fix symptoms instead of causes ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Accept instability as \"normal\" ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"}],"nodeType":"unordered-list"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Trends change that perspective.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"How to prepare test data for accurate test execution trends","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Before analyzing trends, test data must be structured correctly.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Two ReportPortal features are critical here:","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"1. Consistent launch names","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Launch names should represent what is being tested, not when it is tested.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"E2E Regression – good example\nE2E_Regression_2025_02_14_18_32 – bad example","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Build numbers, branches, environments, and versions should be stored as attributes, not embedded in launch names. This allows ReportPortal to group launches correctly and compare them over time.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"2.
